name: Rust license platform (Axum + React)
description: Containerized licensing API in Rust (Axum) with a React frontend, MongoDB persistence, JWT sessions, and signed machine verification headers.
longDescription: This stack delivers a Podman/Docker-friendly licensing service: a Rust Axum API with OpenAPI exposure, MongoDB for persistence, and a React SPA for user flows. Verified users manage license keys through authenticated endpoints; machine verification uses timestamped HMAC signatures on dedicated headers. Development and production compose files split concerns while keeping health checks and build/test scripts reproducible.
features: Health, readiness, and version endpoints for orchestration
Registration, email verification, login, and session management
License issue/verify flows with machine attestation headers
Compose-based dev/prod stacks with documented ports
Automated frontend and backend test runners included in the project
highlights: Strongly typed Rust service boundary with explicit signing roles
Clear separation between browser sessions and machine API verification
challenges: Dual client surfaces (humans in browsers vs machines calling APIs) multiply edge cases around authentication, replay, and clock skew.
solutions: Document timestamped HMAC verification, require production secrets for JWT and machine signing keys, and keep OpenAPI as the contract for integrators.
security: JWT signing secret required in production
Dedicated machine signing secret for X-Signature verification
License digest pepper configurable per environment
Explicit guidance: never commit secrets; use env files or orchestration secrets
